title: 2018 Census External Data Quality Panel: Data sources for key 2018 Census individual variables  | Stats NZ
content:
## 2018 Census External Data Quality Panel: Data sources for key 2018 Census individual variables
17 February 2020, 10:00am
Access a summary of the data sources for key 2018 Census individual variables through the interactive graphs produced by the External Data Quality Panel (EDQP), supported by Stats NZ.
- Graphs showing data sources for key 2018 Census individual variables
- How the data has been produced
The graphs supplement Final report of the 2018 Census External Data Quality Panel by summarising the sources of data for key 2018 Census individual variables.
Due to lower participation in the 2018 Census, data from alternative sources and statistical imputation methods were used to add characteristics of people counted in the 2018 Census. The graphs show the relative proportion of data that has come from each of the sources that were used to supplement data from 2018 Census forms for the variable. The sources are:
The relative contribution of data from alternative sources plays an important part in the Quality Assurance Framework. Metric 1 (Data sources and coverage) is calculated using the relative contribution from each source as an input. See the Data quality assurance for 2018 Census for more information.
The graphs highlight the extent to which coverage can vary across population groups and regions.

## Graphs showing data sources for key 2018 Census individual variables
Select a variable from the drop-down list to view three graphs for each key 2018 Census individual variable:
- Data source by region
- Data source by lowest responding SA2 (statistical area 2) in each region
- Data source by level 1 ethnicity. (Note: Our analysis excludes ‘Other ethnicity’ and ‘Residual categories’.)
Select the 'Tables' and 'Download' tabs to view and download the data behind each graph.

## How the data has been produced
The lowest responding SA2 in each region has been determined by calculating the relative proportion of data sourced from a 2018 Census form from each SA2 within a regional council area.  Extreme outliers are removed by applying a threshold for the number of people in the subject population in each SA2. The SA2 with the lowest proportion is then selected. The threshold is 300 for all variables except Job search methods which has a threshold of 30. This is because the subject population for this variable is significantly smaller than those for all the other variables.
Fixed random rounding has been applied to the data to protect confidentiality.  As a result, the sum of the proportions for all sources may not add to 100.
